description_long: This object is a vertical roadside speed limit sign featuring a square, white sign face with a bold red circular border and the black numeral '40' centered within. The sign is mounted atop a tall, slender, round metal pole that extends well below the sign face, typical of urban traffic signage. The rear of the sign is plain and unmarked, with a muted gray finish, while the front is highly visible for oncoming traffic. The sign face is likely made of reflective metal or coated aluminum to enhance visibility in various lighting conditions. The pole appears to be constructed from galvanized steel, providing durability and resistance to weather. This type of sign is commonly found along urban streets to indicate a maximum speed limit of 40 kilometers per hour.
description: A tall, round metal pole supports a square white speed limit sign with a red border and the number '40' in black. The sign is designed for roadside placement in urban environments.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the plain gray back of the sign and the full length of the round metal pole.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the sign face and the cylindrical shape of the pole.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the white sign face with a red border and the black numeral '40' clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again highlighting the slim profile of the sign and pole.
